taxonomy: Parageum montanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a member of the genus Parageum, which is a small group of plants within the larger family Apiaceae. Further classification places Parageum montanum within the order Apiales, which includes several families of flowering plants related to the carrot and parsley. The class Magnoliopsida encompasses Parageum montanum, as well as many other flowering plants that produce flowers with three petals and three sepals. This classification is part of the larger kingdom Plantae, which includes all plant species.
